overall_summary: Big gap to reference, but the data shows exactly where it is hiding. Attack these points. Current gap: 36.790s slower than reference. The biggest opportunity is Sector 2 (+24.328s), where 61.4 km/h is being left on the table through the corners. Executing these fixes could recover 3.30s — a completely different lap time.

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 61.4 km/h below reference (4.1 vs 65.5 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Despite a late brake point, apex speed is below reference — car not rotating. Use higher initial brake pressure to pitch the car in, trail off through apex. Hit 66 km/h and the exit speed takes care of itself.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 4.1 km/h vs 65.5 km/h reference (-61.4 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Apex speed is 44.6 km/h below reference (49.8 vs 94.4 km/h). Significant corner exit speed deficit.
priority_actions.instruction: Carry more speed to the apex — target 94 km/h. Take a wider entry to enable a later apex and trust the exit. This corner is where straight-line speed is won or lost.
priority_actions.evidence: Apex: 49.8 km/h vs 94.4 km/h reference (-44.6 km/h).

priority_actions.issue: Throttle pick-up is 30m later than reference after the apex.
priority_actions.instruction: Get back to full throttle 30m earlier. Reference applies throttle at 2710m, driver at 2740m. Trust the apex and commit — late throttle bleeds straight-line speed.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 88.8 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 18% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.

sector_feedback.headline: Minimum corner speed is 61.4 km/h below reference — driver is over-slowing significantly through this sector.
sector_feedback.details: Average throttle application is 17% lower than reference — driver is hesitant getting back on power.
